title: Word Counter & Character Counter
description: Count words, characters, sentences, paragraphs and estimate reading time instantly.

inputs.label: Enter or paste your text
inputs.placeholder: Type or paste your text here...

seo.title: Word Counter & Character Counter - Free Online Tool | ToolVerse
seo.description: Count words, characters, sentences and paragraphs instantly. Free online word counter with reading time estimation.

faq.question: How does the word counter work?
faq.answer: The word counter splits your text by whitespace and counts the resulting segments. It also counts characters, sentences (split by punctuation), and paragraphs (split by blank lines).

faq.question: What is reading time based on?
faq.answer: Reading time is estimated at 200 words per minute, which is the average adult reading speed.

faq.question: Does it count special characters?
faq.answer: Yes, the character counter includes all characters — letters, numbers, symbols, and spaces. The 'Characters (no spaces)' output excludes whitespace.

howToUse: Paste or type your text in the input area. Word count, character count, sentence count, paragraph count and estimated reading time update in real-time as you type.

title: JSON Formatter & Validator

seo.title: JSON Formatter & Validator - Free Online Tool | ToolVerse
seo.description: Format, beautify and validate JSON data online. Free JSON formatter with syntax highlighting, error detection and indentation options.

faq.question: What does the JSON formatter do?
faq.answer: It takes raw or minified JSON text, parses it, and outputs it in a human-readable, properly indented format. It also validates that the JSON is syntactically correct.

faq.question: What happens if my JSON is invalid?
faq.answer: The tool will display an error message indicating what went wrong and approximately where the error is located in your JSON string.

faq.question: Can I choose the indentation level?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose between 2 spaces, 4 spaces, or tab indentation using the dropdown menu.

howToUse: Paste your JSON data into the input area, select your preferred indentation, and click 'Run Tool'. The formatted output will appear below with syntax validation status.

title: Base64 Encode & Decode
description: Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 strings back to plain text instantly.

inputs.label: Enter text or Base64 string
inputs.placeholder: Enter text to encode or Base64 to decode...

seo.title: Base64 Encode & Decode - Free Online Tool | ToolVerse
seo.description: Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 strings online. Free, instant Base64 encoder and decoder tool.

faq.question: What is Base64 encoding?
faq.answer: Base64 is a binary-to-text encoding scheme that represents binary data using 64 ASCII characters. It's commonly used to embed data in URLs, emails, and HTML.

faq.question: Is Base64 encryption?
faq.answer: No. Base64 is an encoding, not encryption. It does not provide any security — anyone can decode a Base64 string. Use it for data transport, not for hiding sensitive information.

faq.question: What characters does Base64 use?
faq.answer: Base64 uses A-Z, a-z, 0-9, + and / (64 characters total), with = used for padding. URL-safe Base64 replaces + with - and / with _.

howToUse: Enter your text in the input area and select 'Encode' or 'Decode' mode. The result updates in real-time as you type.

title: UUID / GUID Generator
description: Generate random UUID v4 identifiers. Bulk generate multiple UUIDs at once.

seo.title: UUID Generator - Generate Random UUID/GUID Online | ToolVerse
seo.description: Generate random UUID v4 identifiers online. Bulk generate multiple UUIDs with options for uppercase and hyphen formatting.

faq.question: What is a UUID?
faq.answer: A UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is a 128-bit identifier that is practically unique across all systems. Version 4 UUIDs are randomly generated and have the format xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx.

faq.question: Are generated UUIDs truly unique?
faq.answer: UUID v4 uses random numbers, making collisions astronomically unlikely. The probability of generating a duplicate is about 1 in 2^122 (5.3 x 10^36).

faq.question: What is the difference between UUID and GUID?
faq.answer: UUID and GUID are essentially the same thing. GUID (Globally Unique Identifier) is Microsoft's term for UUID. They follow the same specification and format.

howToUse: Set the number of UUIDs you want to generate, choose formatting options (uppercase, hyphens), and click 'Run Tool'. Click the copy button to copy all generated UUIDs.

title: Case Converter
description: Convert text between uppercase, lowercase, title case, sentence case, camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case and PascalCase.

inputs.label: Enter your text
inputs.placeholder: Type or paste text to convert...

seo.title: Case Converter - Transform Text Case Online | ToolVerse
seo.description: Convert text between uppercase, lowercase, title case, camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case and more. Free online case converter tool.

faq.question: What case formats are supported?
faq.answer: The tool supports: UPPERCASE, lowercase, Title Case, Sentence case, camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, kebab-case, and CONSTANT_CASE.

faq.question: What is camelCase?
faq.answer: camelCase joins words together with no separator, capitalizing the first letter of each word except the first. Example: 'hello world' becomes 'helloWorld'. It's commonly used for variable names in JavaScript.

faq.question: What is the difference between snake_case and kebab-case?
faq.answer: snake_case uses underscores between words (common in Python and databases), while kebab-case uses hyphens (common in URLs and CSS class names). Both use lowercase letters.

howToUse: Enter your text in the input area, select the target case format from the dropdown, and the converted text will appear in real-time.

faq.answer: Lorem Ipsum is placeholder text used in design and publishing since the 1500s. It helps visualize how content will look without being distracted by readable text.

faq.question: Can I choose how much text to generate?

faq.answer: Yes, the tool keeps the first occurrence of each unique line in its original position and removes subsequent duplicates.

faq.question: What does case-sensitive mean?
faq.answer: When case-sensitive is enabled, 'Hello' and 'hello' are treated as different lines. When disabled, they are treated as duplicates.

faq.question: Does it handle empty lines?
faq.answer: Yes, empty lines are treated like any other line. Multiple consecutive empty lines will be reduced to one.

faq.answer: You can sort A-Z (alphabetical), Z-A (reverse alphabetical), by line length (ascending or descending), reverse the original order, or randomize lines.

faq.question: Is the sort case-sensitive?
faq.answer: The alphabetical sort is case-insensitive by default, so 'Apple' and 'banana' sort naturally without uppercase letters being prioritized.

faq.question: Can I sort numbers?
faq.answer: The tool sorts as text by default. For purely numeric lines, alphabetical sort works correctly for same-length numbers. For mixed content, results are sorted lexicographically.

howToUse: Paste your text with one item per line, select your preferred sort method, and click 'Run Tool'.

faq.question: Can I use this to check palindromes?

faq.question: What is a URL slug?
faq.answer: A slug is the URL-friendly version of a title or name. It uses only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens/underscores. For example, 'My Blog Post!' becomes 'my-blog-post'.

faq.question: How does it handle special characters?
faq.answer: Special characters, accents, and symbols are removed. Spaces are converted to hyphens (or underscores). Multiple consecutive separators are collapsed into one.

faq.question: Why are slugs important for SEO?
faq.answer: Clean, readable URLs with relevant keywords help search engines understand page content and improve click-through rates from search results.

howToUse: Enter your text (like a page title), choose hyphen or underscore separator, and the URL slug generates in real-time.

faq.question: How does the diff work?
faq.answer: The tool compares both texts line by line. Lines present only in the original are marked with '-' (removed), lines present only in the modified text are marked with '+' (added), and unchanged lines are shown without markers.

faq.question: Can it compare large texts?
faq.answer: Yes, the tool handles texts of any size. Very large texts may take a moment to process.

faq.question: Does it show word-level differences?

title: URL Encode / Decode
description: Encode special characters for URLs or decode percent-encoded URL strings.

seo.title: URL Encode / Decode - Free Online Tool | ToolVerse
seo.description: Encode or decode URL strings online. Convert special characters to percent-encoding and back. Free URL encoder decoder.

faq.question: What is URL encoding?
faq.answer: URL encoding converts special characters into a format that can be safely transmitted in URLs. For example, spaces become %20, and & becomes %26.

faq.question: When should I URL encode?
faq.answer: URL encode when passing special characters in query parameters, form data, or any part of a URL that might contain reserved characters.

faq.question: What is the difference between encodeURI and encodeURIComponent?
faq.answer: This tool uses encodeURIComponent which encodes all special characters. encodeURI preserves URL structure characters like :, /, and ?.

howToUse: Enter your text or URL-encoded string, select Encode or Decode mode, and the result updates in real-time.

title: HTML Entity Encode / Decode
description: Encode special characters as HTML entities or decode HTML entities back to text.

seo.title: HTML Entity Encode / Decode - Free Online Tool | ToolVerse
seo.description: Encode special characters as HTML entities or decode HTML entities. Prevent XSS and display HTML code safely.

faq.question: What are HTML entities?
faq.answer: HTML entities are special codes used to display reserved HTML characters. For example, < becomes < and & becomes &. They prevent browsers from interpreting text as HTML.

faq.question: Why encode HTML entities?
faq.answer: Encoding prevents XSS attacks and ensures that characters like <, >, and & display as text rather than being interpreted as HTML tags or entities.

faq.question: Which characters get encoded?
faq.answer: The most commonly encoded characters are: & (&), < (<), > (>), " ("), and ' (').

howToUse: Enter text with special characters (or HTML entities to decode), select mode, and the result updates in real-time.

title: Hash Generator (SHA-256, SHA-1, SHA-512)

seo.description: Generate SHA-256, SHA-1, SHA-384 and SHA-512 hash digests online. Free cryptographic hash generator using Web Crypto API.

faq.question: What is a hash function?
faq.answer: A hash function takes input data and produces a fixed-size string of characters (the hash). It's a one-way function — you can't reverse a hash back to the original data.

faq.question: Which algorithm should I use?
faq.answer: SHA-256 is recommended for most purposes. SHA-1 is considered weak for security. SHA-384 and SHA-512 provide longer digests for higher security needs.

faq.question: Is this done client-side?
faq.answer: Yes, all hashing is performed in your browser using the Web Crypto API. Your data never leaves your device.

howToUse: Enter the text you want to hash, select the algorithm, and click 'Run Tool'. The cryptographic hash will appear below.

faq.question: What regex flags are supported?
faq.answer: Common flags: g (global - find all matches), i (case-insensitive), m (multiline - ^ and $ match line boundaries), s (dotAll - . matches newlines).

faq.question: How do I see capture groups?
faq.answer: Use parentheses in your pattern to create capture groups. The tool displays each match with its captured groups listed separately.

faq.question: What regex flavor is used?
faq.answer: The tool uses JavaScript's RegExp engine, which supports most common regex features including lookaheads, named groups, and Unicode escapes.

howToUse: Enter your regex pattern and flags, paste test text, and click 'Run Tool'. All matches and capture groups will be displayed.

title: JWT Decoder
description: Decode JSON Web Tokens (JWT) to inspect header and payload data without verification.

seo.title: JWT Decoder - Decode JSON Web Tokens Online | ToolVerse
seo.description: Decode JWT tokens to view header and payload data. Inspect claims, expiration, and issuer. Free online JWT decoder.

faq.question: What is a JWT?
faq.answer: A JSON Web Token (JWT) is a compact, URL-safe token format used for securely transmitting information between parties. It consists of three parts: header, payload, and signature.

faq.question: Does this verify the signature?
faq.answer: No, this tool only decodes the header and payload. It does not verify the cryptographic signature. Never trust a JWT's contents without proper server-side verification.

faq.question: Is it safe to paste my JWT here?
faq.answer: Yes, all processing is done client-side in your browser. Your token is never sent to any server. However, avoid sharing JWTs in public as they may contain sensitive claims.

howToUse: Paste your JWT token in the input field. The decoded header and payload will display in real-time with formatted JSON.

title: Color Converter

seo.title: Color Converter - HEX to RGB to HSL | ToolVerse
seo.description: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, and HSL formats instantly. Free online color converter for designers and developers.

faq.question: What color formats are supported?
faq.answer: The tool supports HEX (#ff5733 or ff5733), RGB (255, 87, 51), and HSL (9, 100%, 66%) color formats.

faq.question: What is the difference between RGB and HSL?
faq.answer: RGB defines colors by Red, Green, Blue values (0-255). HSL uses Hue (0-360°), Saturation (0-100%), and Lightness (0-100%), which is more intuitive for color adjustments.

faq.question: Does it support shorthand HEX?
faq.answer: Yes, 3-digit shorthand HEX codes like #f00 are expanded to 6-digit format (#ff0000) automatically.

howToUse: Enter a color value, select the input format, and all color representations (HEX, RGB, HSL) update in real-time.

title: CSS Unit Converter
description: Convert between CSS units: px, rem, em, and pt. Set your base font size for accurate conversions.

seo.title: CSS Unit Converter - px to rem to em | ToolVerse
seo.description: Convert between CSS units: px, rem, em, and pt. Set custom base size for accurate conversions. Free online CSS unit calculator.

faq.question: What is the base font size?
faq.answer: The base font size (default 16px in browsers) determines rem and em conversions. 1rem = 1 × base font size. Change this if your project uses a different root font size.

faq.question: What is the difference between rem and em?
faq.answer: rem is relative to the root element's font size, while em is relative to the parent element's font size. rem is more predictable in nested elements.

faq.question: When should I use rem vs px?
faq.answer: Use rem for scalable, accessible designs that respect user font size preferences. Use px for fixed elements where exact pixel dimensions matter (borders, shadows).

howToUse: Enter a value, select the source unit, set your base font size if needed, and all equivalent values update in real-time.

title: JSON Minifier
description: Minify JSON by removing all whitespace and formatting. See how much space you save.

seo.title: JSON Minifier - Compress JSON Online | ToolVerse
seo.description: Minify JSON by removing whitespace and formatting. See character savings and compression ratio. Free online JSON compressor.

faq.question: What does minifying JSON do?
faq.answer: Minification removes all unnecessary whitespace, newlines, and indentation from JSON while keeping it valid. This reduces file size for faster network transfers.

faq.question: Is the minified JSON still valid?
faq.answer: Yes, minified JSON is fully valid. It contains the same data structure and values — only formatting whitespace is removed.

faq.question: How much space can I save?
faq.answer: Savings depend on the original formatting. Typically you can save 20-60% of characters by minifying well-formatted JSON.

howToUse: Paste your formatted JSON, click 'Run Tool', and get the minified version with statistics showing how much space was saved.

faq.answer: This is a lightweight parser covering the most common Markdown features. For complex documents with tables, footnotes, or custom extensions, a full parser like marked.js is recommended.

title: Number Base Converter

faq.question: What is hexadecimal?
faq.answer: Hexadecimal (base 16) uses digits 0-9 and letters A-F. It's commonly used in computing for memory addresses, color codes, and byte representation.

faq.question: Can I convert large numbers?

title: Unix Timestamp Converter
description: Convert Unix timestamps to human-readable dates and vice versa. Supports seconds and milliseconds.

seo.title: Unix Timestamp Converter - Epoch to Date | ToolVerse
seo.description: Convert Unix timestamps to dates and dates to timestamps. Supports seconds and milliseconds. Free online epoch converter.

faq.question: What is a Unix timestamp?
faq.answer: A Unix timestamp is the number of seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC (the Unix epoch). It's widely used in computing for date/time storage.

faq.question: Does it handle milliseconds?
faq.answer: Yes, if the timestamp is longer than 10 digits, it's automatically treated as milliseconds. Otherwise it's treated as seconds.

faq.question: What date formats are accepted?
faq.answer: You can enter dates in ISO 8601 format (2023-11-14T22:13:20Z), or common date strings that JavaScript's Date parser recognizes.

howToUse: Enter a Unix timestamp (to convert to date) or a date string (to get the timestamp), select the mode, and results update in real-time.

faq.question: How do Open Graph tags help?
faq.answer: Open Graph tags control how your page appears when shared on social media (Facebook, LinkedIn). They set the title, description, and image for social previews.

faq.question: What is Open Graph?
faq.answer: Open Graph is a protocol created by Facebook that controls how your content appears when shared on social media platforms. It defines the title, description, image, and URL shown in social cards.

faq.question: What size should the OG image be?
faq.answer: The recommended OG image size is 1200×630 pixels. This works well across Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, and other platforms.

faq.question: How do I test my actual page's OG tags?
faq.answer: After adding OG tags to your page, use Facebook's Sharing Debugger or Twitter's Card Validator to test how your real page appears when shared.

howToUse: Fill in the OG fields (title, description, image URL, page URL, site name) and see the generated meta tags plus a text preview of how the social card will look.

title: robots.txt Generator
description: Generate a robots.txt file for your website. Control search engine crawling with allow/disallow rules.

seo.title: robots.txt Generator - Free Online Tool | ToolVerse
seo.description: Generate robots.txt files for your website. Control crawling, set allow/disallow rules, and add sitemap reference. Free robots.txt builder.

faq.question: What is robots.txt?
faq.answer: robots.txt is a text file placed at your website's root that tells search engine crawlers which pages to crawl and which to skip. It's part of the Robots Exclusion Protocol.

faq.question: Does robots.txt block pages from appearing in search?
faq.answer: No, robots.txt only prevents crawling, not indexing. Pages can still appear in search results if other pages link to them. Use meta robots noindex to prevent indexing.

faq.question: Where should I place robots.txt?
faq.answer: robots.txt must be placed at the root of your domain: https://example.com/robots.txt. It won't work in subdirectories.

howToUse: Set the user-agent, add allow/disallow paths, optionally add your sitemap URL and crawl delay. The robots.txt content generates in real-time.

title: Sitemap XML Generator
description: Generate a sitemap.xml file from a list of URLs. Set change frequency and priority for each URL.

seo.title: Sitemap XML Generator - Free Online Tool | ToolVerse
seo.description: Generate sitemap.xml from a URL list. Set change frequency and priority. Free online sitemap generator for better SEO.

faq.question: What is a sitemap.xml?
faq.answer: A sitemap.xml file lists all pages on your website that you want search engines to discover. It helps crawlers find and index your pages more efficiently.

faq.question: How important is the priority value?
faq.answer: Priority is a hint to search engines about relative importance of pages on YOUR site. It doesn't affect ranking versus other websites. Use 0.8-1.0 for key pages, 0.5 for normal pages.

faq.question: Where do I submit my sitemap?
faq.answer: Submit your sitemap in Google Search Console, Bing Webmaster Tools, and reference it in your robots.txt file with the Sitemap: directive.

howToUse: Enter your URLs (one per line), set change frequency and priority, and click 'Run Tool' to generate the XML sitemap.

title: HTTP Status Code Reference
description: Search and browse all HTTP status codes with descriptions. Quick reference for web developers.

seo.title: HTTP Status Codes Reference - Complete List | ToolVerse
seo.description: Searchable HTTP status code reference. Find any status code with description and meaning. Complete HTTP response codes list.

faq.question: What are HTTP status codes?
faq.answer: HTTP status codes are three-digit numbers returned by servers to indicate the result of a request. 2xx means success, 3xx means redirection, 4xx means client error, and 5xx means server error.

faq.question: What is the most common error code?
faq.answer: 404 (Not Found) is the most common client error, meaning the requested page doesn't exist. 500 (Internal Server Error) is the most common server error.

faq.question: What is the difference between 301 and 302?
faq.answer: 301 is a permanent redirect (search engines transfer link equity). 302 is a temporary redirect (search engines keep indexing the original URL).

howToUse: Type a status code number or keyword to search. Leave empty to see all codes. Results update in real-time as you type.
